name: Hyrrokkin
description: Hyrrokkin is a giantess (Jötunn) in Norse mythology known for her immense strength and the role she played in Baldr's funeral. When Baldr, the beloved son of Odin and Frigg, was slain, his magnificent ship, Hringhorni, the largest ship ever built, needed to be launched for his funeral pyre. However, no god, not even Thor, possessed the strength to move the massive vessel. The gods then summoned Hyrrokkin, who arrived riding a wolf and using vipers as reins. With a single push, she launched the ship with such force that the earth trembled and the rollers beneath the ship burst into flames. Thor, angered by her strength and the disruption, almost struck her down with his hammer, Mjolnir, but was stopped by the other gods. Her role highlights the power and influence that even beings outside the Aesir and Vanir could wield. While her origins remain somewhat mysterious, her appearance at Baldr's funeral solidifies her presence in Norse cosmological events. She represents raw, untamed strength, a force that even the gods had to acknowledge and, at times, rely upon. Her story serves as a reminder of the complex power dynamics within the Norse pantheon and the respect, albeit sometimes grudging, that was given to beings of great power, regardless of their origin. Hyrrokkin's tale is a brief but impactful one, showcasing a raw, almost chaotic strength that momentarily overshadowed even the gods themselves.
subtitle: Giantess of immense strength.

keyMyths.title: Launching Hringhorni
keyMyths.description: Hyrrokkin launches Baldr's ship, Hringhorni, with incredible force, showcasing her immense strength. Her actions cause the earth to tremble and anger Thor.

keyMyths.title: Arrival at the Funeral
keyMyths.description: Hyrrokkin arrives at Baldr's funeral riding a wolf and using vipers as reins, demonstrating her wild and untamed nature. This bold entrance impresses and slightly intimidates the gods.

keyMyths.title: Thor's Anger
keyMyths.description: Thor is enraged by Hyrrokkin's strength and the chaos she causes, and nearly attacks her. The other gods intervene, preventing him from striking her with Mjolnir.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: A primary source for Norse mythology, including the story of Hyrrokkin in Gylfaginning.
